description: A clean USCIS designed slogan graphic on a pale blue gradient background watermarked with a faint, large 'DEPARTMENT OF ... HOMELAND ...' seal/wreath behind the text. The headline, set in a heavy dark-navy serif font at upper left, reads: 'Green cards and visas are a privilege, not a right.' with the word 'privilege' rendered in bold italic and underlined with a hand-drawn red swoosh for emphasis. Below the headline are two overlapping specimen identity documents shown as photographs: a U.S. 'PERMANENT RESIDENT' green card (sample bearing name 'TEST V SPECIMEN', USCIS# 000-000-310, Category IR1, Country of Birth Bahamas, Date of Birth 17 AUG 1958, with a Statue of Liberty/flag background and a sample headshot of a man) and a U.S. 'IMMIGRANT VISA' (Issuing Post US Consulate General London, surname TRAVELER, given name HAPPYPERSON, Category IR1, with a sample headshot of a woman and a machine-readable zone reading 'VIUSATRAVELER<<HAPPYPERSON<<...'). The propaganda/messaging technique is a stern legal-warning framing that immigration status is revocable and conditional, supporting the tweet's point that 'rigorous security vetting does not end' and lawbreakers will 'lose your privileges.' Target audience: visa and green-card holders / immigrants, signaling enforcement and the threat of revocation.
notable_text: Green cards and visas are a privilege, not a right.
